summary:
Helmets cells result from mechanical destruction of red blood cells during passage through damaged small vessels, which occurs primarily in microangiopathic hemolytic anemias including hemolytic uremic syndrome. These cells are therefore characteristically seen in hemolytic uremic syndrome, not in other listed disorders involving different morphological changes such as spherocytosis or acanthocytosis.\boxed{{A}}

The correct association between helmet cells and their underlying pathology lies in microangiopathic processes causing intravascular hemolysis; this directly links them to hemolytic uremic syndrome via its mechanism of endothelium-mediated RBC rupture and fragment production. Thus, option A accurately represents the pathological basis observed clinically.
